component.name: Paladin
component.summary: Generate one-time passwords from your TOTP and HOTP accounts

description.p: Paladin is a privacy-focused one-time password authenticator that runs entirely on your computer. It supports the time-based (TOTP, RFC 6238) and counter-based (HOTP, RFC 4226) algorithms used by major web services, password managers, and 2-factor authentication providers.

ul.li: Store TOTP and HOTP accounts in an encrypted vault on disk
Search-and-copy workflow with optional clipboard auto-clear
Encrypted vaults use Argon2id key derivation and XChaCha20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption
Add accounts manually, by pasting an otpauth:// URI, or by scanning a QR code from the clipboard
Import and export accounts in otpauth-JSON, Aegis-plaintext, and encrypted Paladin bundle formats
Auto-lock the vault after a configurable idle interval
No network access, no telemetry, no account or cloud sync

screenshot.caption: Paladin's account list with a TOTP code being copied

description.p: First public GTK4 + libadwaita release of Paladin alongside the v0.1 CLI and TUI. Adds search-and-copy of TOTP and HOTP accounts, account add via manual entry / otpauth:// URI paste / clipboard QR scan, encrypted-vault unlock with Argon2id, opt-in auto-lock and clipboard auto-clear, import and export across otpauth-JSON / Aegis / encrypted Paladin bundles, and live-apply preferences.
